nodes.label: useHeroFullscreen Hook
nodes.rationale: Orientation lock/unlock kept inside useHeroFullscreen because the hook already owns enter/exit and the fullscreenchange listener; lock only succeeds inside a fullscreen context so sequencing matters. A CSS transform:rotate(90deg) portrait hack was rejected because it breaks pointer/scroll coordinates and YouTube controls. Unlock is driven from the fullscreenchange sync so Esc/gesture exits also release the lock.

nodes.label: Screen Orientation API
nodes.rationale: Feature-detected (screen.orientation with lock method) and failures swallowed via .catch; iOS Safari and desktop reject the lock, which is benign. No CSS fake-fullscreen fallback for iOS (out of scope).

nodes.label: useScrollFocus Hook
nodes.rationale: Reports whether an element is centered using an IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-47% 0px -47% 0px'. Chosen over a scroll listener + distance math (intersection is computed off the main thread, no per-frame cost) and over CSS animation-timeline:view() (unreliable iOS Safari support as of 2026). No-op on hover-capable pointers via matchMedia('(hover: none)'). Band -47% kept above the 24px gutter to avoid 'nothing highlighted' flicker.
